title: Star Wars Missions 13: Prisoner of the Nikto Pirates
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:Missions13.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars Missions 13:
Prisoner of the Nikto Pirates'' |author=[[Dave Wolverton]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[September]] [[1998]]''[[Star Wars Missions 13: Prisoner of the Nikto Pirates]]'' |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages=70 |isbn=0590592777 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=c. [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5]]–[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]''[[Star Wars Missions 13: Prisoner of the Nikto Pirates]]'' and the next three ''Star Wars Missions'' books tell a continuous story that is set after the Evacuation of Yavin, during the construction of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], thus placing the books between [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5]]–[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. |series=''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' |preceded by=''[[Star Wars Missions 12: The Vactooine Disaster]]'' |followed by=''[[Star Wars Missions 14: The Monsters of Dweem]]'' }} '''''Star Wars Missions 13: Prisoner of the Nikto Pirates''''' is the thirteenth adventure book in the ''[[Star Wars Missions]]'' young-reader series and [[roleplaying game]]. It was published by [[Scholastic]] in [[September]] [[1998]]. {{Book-stub}} ==Publisher's summary== ''The ''Millennium Falcon'' has been damaged and captured by bloodthirsty Nikto pirates. You are trapped in the pirates' ''Predator''-class vessel…with only one chance to break free. Time is already running out. Your mission: to steal the parts needed to fix the ''Falcon'', repair your ship, and make a daring escape.'' ''May the Force be with you.'' ==Plot summary== [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] travel to a [[Skrilling salvage ship]], in search of supplies for the establishment of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. While bartering there, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] encounters the ancient droid [[B-1D4]], an expert in gender relations. Wondeefor engages in a datalink with 3PO, which greatly enhances the protocol droid. Organa, impressed with Wondeefor, buys her. The ''Falcon'' then leaves for [[Dweem]], but its hyperspace engine is damaged, leaving it stranded. The ''[[M'hendosat]]'' discovers the ''Falcon'', and its captain, [[Kassihm]], orders that Solo's ship be salvaged and torn apart. Solo, once aboard, plans to trap the Nikto pirates and repair his ship. One of the Rebels escapes the Nikto craft and roams free on the ''M'hendosat'', wreaking havoc, while their compatriots are left behind to deal with the boarding party. Those left are all knocked out by [[Bothan stun spore]]s and thrown in the brig. Meanwhile, the escapee sabotages the Nikto ships. The Rebel powers up the droids, which have been left behind. Beewondeefor begins convincing the ''M'hendosat''{{'s}} computer to deactivate all security systems and unlock the cell doors. The Rebel steals all the parts required to fix the ''Falcon'', before saving his companions and escaping. During the Rebels' escape, the ship's power core sustained significant damage and was destroyed. Kassihm, however, escaped. ==Appearances== {{App |characters= *[[4-8C]] {{1st}} *[[B-1D4]] {{1st}} *[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] *[[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] *[[Dilrath]] {{1st}} *[[Kassihm]] {{1st}}
